rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FIORANI LIVING TRUST DTD. 1/25/02 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner And Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment And To Enter New Judgment Enforcing Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Vacate Void Judgment is denied. The judgment is not void. There is no rule that every court case must result in a judgment reduced to the specific formula A owes B X dollars. Many cases do not involve dollar judgments, for example quiet title and unlawful detainer actions, divorce, conservatorship, and guardianship petitions, petitions to approve accountings, and injunction actions, to name just a few. Judgments in such actions are nonetheless valid and enforceable. CCP section 577.5 merely requires that a money judgment be expressed in dollars and cents, rejecting fractions. Section 664.6 expressly allows the court to "enter judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement," which is exactly what this judgment does. To the extent the settlement included payment obligations, the judgment, by incorporating the settlement, does state the terms, including who owes how much to whom and when. Section 664.6 would not allow the court to alter the terms of the settlement in the manner requested by the moving party by omitting material terms and accelerating payment obligations. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Wednesday, August 26, 2015.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Jennifer Kwok's motion for summary adjudication as to the Eighth Cause of Action for Quiet Title is denied. There are trial issues of material fact regarding the delivery and validity of the 2008 Deed. Evidence Code Section 662 is only applicable where the dispute concerns the beneficial title, not legal title as in this case. (Murray v. Murray (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 1062, 1067.) Civil Code Section 683.2, subdivision (c) is also inapplicable here because it expressly deals with the several of a joint tentancy with right of survivorship, not tenancy in common. There are triable issues of material fact as to the decedent's intent in making the financial contributions to Respondent. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than August 26, 2015, at 4:00 p.m. Argument would then be scheduled on September 1, 2015 (Tuesday), at 2:30 p.m.
